regimeSub: Reflation regime: CPI at 4.2% 3-yr high forces Warsh Fed to drop cutting bias and flag September hike even as Iran ceasefire cools oil.
themes: US-Iran deal halts attacks -> Brent back below $74, energy spike fading but CPI lag keeps prints hot
New Fed chair Warsh kills cut bias -> market now pricing hike risk, real rates still negative at -0.6%
AI/energy capex supercycle -> equities make new highs (SPX 7440, Nasdaq +2%) despite hawkish Fed
Gold near $3982 + Cook firing fight -> markets hedge Fed-independence erosion and sticky inflation

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 3.63% policy vs 4.2% CPI = -0.6% real; policy still loose despite hawkish rhetoric, hike justified.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bp, mildly positive; bear-steepening risk if Warsh hikes into sticky inflation.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; VIX 17.6 and equity highs signal no credit stress, complacent to hike risk.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.65, calm post-ceasefire; underpricing September Fed hike and Grantham crash warnings.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73.85, down hard on US-Iran deal; supply-surge narrative vs geopolitical premium unwind.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $3981, near records; hedging negative real rates, Fed-independence risk and sticky CPI.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1543 weak won; JPY at 162 too — Asia FX soft as USD holds and BOJ/BOK lag.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: SK Hynix warning flags memory cyclicality; AI demand still driving but Micron risk rising.

headline: As of 2026-06-30 the world is a sticky-inflation reflation: CPI at a 3-year-high 4.2% and a hawkish Warsh Fed collide with an Iran ceasefire that pulled Brent under $74, while equities print records and gold hovers near $3,980.
